stars.description: 26m function call model that runs on incredibly small devices
stars.highlights: A compact 26M parameter model optimized for function calling on resource-constrained devices, enabling on-device AI execution. It leverages Gemma architecture and is designed for low-latency, privacy-preserving inference.
stars.worthReading: It demonstrates how to run capable LLMs on tiny hardware, opening up edge AI applications like IoT and mobile assistants.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is moderate (534 stars, rising but not explosive), relevance is high (on-device LLM for function calling aligns with agent and deployment trends), concreteness is high (working model with clear specs).

stars.highlights: This plugin enables Tailscale authentication for Datasette, allowing users to restrict access to their Datasette instances to Tailscale network members. It leverages Tailscale's identity and access controls for seamless, secure sharing.
stars.worthReading: For Datasette users already on Tailscale, this plugin provides a simple yet powerful way to add authentication without managing separate user databases, making it ideal for internal tools and collaborative data exploration.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is low (2 stars, no traction); relevance is moderate (infra/tooling for Datasette, but niche); concreteness is high (working plugin with clear purpose).

stars.description: How FastFast can you pull from Hugging Face?
stars.highlights: A simple Python script to benchmark download speeds from Hugging Face Hub, measuring how fast you can pull models and datasets. Useful for diagnosing network performance and optimizing CI/CD pipelines.
stars.worthReading: If you frequently download from Hugging Face, this tool helps identify speed bottlenecks and compare providers or regions.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness is low (24 stars, no clear traction), relevance is moderate (infra/tooling for AI devs), concreteness is high (working benchmark script).

stars.description: DeepSeek 4 Flash local inference engine for Metal and CUDA
stars.highlights: ds4 is a lightweight, high-performance local inference engine for DeepSeek 4 Flash, supporting both Metal (Apple Silicon) and CUDA (NVIDIA GPUs). It offers efficient model execution with minimal dependencies, making it ideal for on-device AI applications.
stars.worthReading: This repo provides a practical, optimized solution for running DeepSeek 4 Flash locally, which is valuable for developers seeking to deploy LLMs on edge devices without cloud dependencies.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness (3): 8k stars and rising, new release; Relevance (4): directly addresses local LLM inference, a frontier AI topic; Concreteness (2): working engine with clear hardware support.

stars.highlights: Serve-sim provides a simple CLI to run Apple Simulators headlessly, similar to `npx serve` for web servers. It enables launching iOS simulators without the Simulator.app GUI, useful for CI/CD and automated testing.
stars.worthReading: For AI engineers building agentic iOS apps or testing on-device AI models, this tool simplifies simulator management in headless environments.
stars.scoreReason: Freshness 2 (1k stars, rising but not explosive), Relevance 3 (agent-related tooling for iOS testing), Concreteness 1 (specific CLI tool, but limited to Apple ecosystem).
